steps.title: Identify what the blank requires
steps.content: The blank completes “as [blank] sediment” within a subordinate clause. The subject of “expand and contract” is “rivers,” which is plural and non-human, so the blank needs a third-person plural pronoun paired with a matching verb.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘they accumulate’: “They” is third-person plural and correctly agrees with the plural noun “rivers.” ✓
  • Choice B — ‘one accumulates’: “One” is singular; “rivers” is plural—pronoun-antecedent agreement error. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘it accumulates’: “It” is singular; “rivers” is plural—same agreement error. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘we accumulate’: “We” is first-person and implies the speaker is a river, which makes no sense here. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
